references.name: Squarespace
references.takeaway: Use extra-soft box shadows (30px+ blur, 8% opacity) on all elevated elements to create the impression of content cards floating on a water surface with gentle diffused shadows beneath.

influencedElements.element: All cards (project cards, kanban cards, idea cards, stat panels)
influencedElements.implementation: CSS --shadow-float: 0 8px 32px rgba(58,53,53,0.06) applied to every card surface. The 32px blur radius with 6% opacity creates the paper-floating-on-water effect. On hover, shadow deepens to 0 16px 48px with 12% opacity to enhance the floating sensation.
Each panel uses var(--shadow-float) with an additional soft pink-tinted shadow (rgba(212,136,154,0.08)) that gives an almost watercolor edge-bleed impression where the shadow meets the background.

The watercolor-wash-wipe transition uses a smooth cross-dissolve approach inspired by Squarespace's template preview morphing — a radial gradient overlay sweeps across the viewport with soft edges, never a hard cut.

references.name: Glossier
references.takeaway: Apply radial gradient washes as section backgrounds that fade from a tinted pastel center to white edges, mimicking the way watercolor pigment bleeds outward from a wet brush drop.

influencedElements.element: Dashboard stat panels (wash-pink, wash-sage, wash-lavender, wash-peach)
influencedElements.implementation: Each stat panel uses a linear-gradient with a pastel color at 12% opacity fading to white at 80%, plus a radial gradient ::before pseudo-element that appears on hover — mimicking a second layer of watercolor wash bleeding from a corner.

Vertical linear gradients that start with a tinted pastel at the top (6-8% opacity), dissolve through white, and bleed into a different pastel at the bottom (3% opacity) — each section transitions into the next as a continuous watercolor wash.

Each idea card has a ::before pseudo-element with a two-color linear gradient at 8% opacity, giving every card a unique watercolor blend. Eight different blend combinations ensure no two cards look alike, inspired by Glossier's gradient washes.

Full pill-radius (border-radius: 999px) on buttons, inputs, search fields, tags, and toggle tracks — no sharp corners anywhere in interactive surfaces, following Glossier's organic rounded shape language.

references.name: Goop
references.takeaway: Let images bleed slightly outside their grid columns to overlap adjacent text areas by 20-40px, creating the soft irregular edges of watercolor where pigment seeps beyond the intended boundary.

influencedElements.implementation: Each whiteboard node has a wb-splatter div positioned at inset: -8px that extends 8px beyond the node's content boundary with blur(8px) filter — the colored splatter bleeds outside the card edge exactly as watercolor seeps beyond an intended boundary.

Entity cards have a schema-entity-wash positioned at inset: -6px that extends beyond the entity panel with blur(6px), creating the impression that the pastel background wash has bled slightly past the card edges into the surrounding canvas.

The AI chat uses an editorial long-form layout with generous line-height (1.75 on message text using Cormorant serif) and wide message padding — inspired by Goop's editorial typography. The handwritten letter format with serif body text at generous leading creates the editorial quality.

The button ::before pseudo-element extends inset: -4px beyond the button boundary, so the radial gradient wash bleeds 4px past the button's visual edge on hover — a micro version of Goop's content-bleeding-beyond-grid principle.
